scope.time_coverage: 2017-2024 initial seed, with several foundational pre-2017 anchors where useful

paper_schema.contribution_type: Algorithm | Theory | Framework | Analysis | Benchmark | Architecture | Task
paper_schema.storyline_structure: How the paper motivates the problem
paper_schema.logical_flow: Argument sequence used to persuade reviewers

archetype_library.name: Observation -> Method
archetype_library.definition: The paper starts from a concrete failure mode, empirical phenomenon, or conceptual observation and derives a method around it.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Make the observation undeniable and show that every method component follows from it.

archetype_library.name: Theory -> Algorithm
archetype_library.definition: The paper introduces or uses a formal principle, bound, objective, or theorem to derive a practical algorithm.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Connect the formal result to a concrete algorithmic advantage rather than leaving theory as decoration.

archetype_library.name: Algorithm + Theoretical Analysis
archetype_library.definition: The paper is primarily a method paper, but includes theory to explain, bound, or justify the algorithm.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Reduce the risk that the method looks like heuristic engineering by giving an explanatory property.

archetype_library.name: Framework Unification
archetype_library.definition: The paper reframes several methods, tasks, or capabilities under a unified abstraction.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Show that the framework explains prior work and enables something that isolated methods do not.

archetype_library.name: Benchmark / Dataset Construction
archetype_library.definition: The main contribution is a dataset, benchmark, evaluation protocol, or leaderboard that exposes missing capabilities.
typical_storyline: current benchmarks are saturated or narrow
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Convince reviewers that the benchmark measures a real capability and will change community behavior.

archetype_library.name: Empirical Analysis
archetype_library.definition: The paper's primary contribution is systematic evidence, scaling behavior, diagnosis, or empirical understanding.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Make the study broad and controlled enough that the conclusions are hard to dismiss as anecdotal.

archetype_library.name: Model Architecture Design
archetype_library.definition: The paper proposes a new architecture or architectural component as the main technical object.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Tie each architectural choice to a failure mode and prove the components are necessary.

archetype_library.name: New Task Formulation
archetype_library.definition: The paper argues that the community should solve a newly defined task, setting, or problem formulation.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Show that the task is important, non-reducible to existing tasks, and measurable.

archetype_library.name: Comparative Analysis
archetype_library.definition: The paper compares families of methods or models to derive practical and scientific conclusions.
archetype_library.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Avoid shallow benchmarking by making the comparison axes explicit and controlled.

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.research_problem: Sequence transduction models were limited by recurrent/convolutional computation and long-range dependency handling.
logical_flow: argue parallelization and path length benefits
papers.theory_pattern: Complexity and path-length comparison rather than theorem-heavy analysis.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A simple architecture removes major sequence-modeling constraints while improving quality and efficiency.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.research_problem: Prior language representations were limited by unidirectional or shallow bidirectional pre-training.
storyline_structure: masked language modeling enables deep bidirectionality
papers.theory_pattern: No central theorem; persuasion comes from objective design and broad transfer evidence.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A simple pre-training objective produces large and consistent gains across many NLU tasks.

papers.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
papers.research_problem: Convolution was assumed essential for vision backbones, while pure Transformer scaling for images was unclear.
storyline_structure: vision still relies on CNN inductive bias
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theorem; the architecture argument is empirical and scaling-driven.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Show that a minimal architecture change is viable only when paired with scale, explaining both success and limitation.

papers.title: The Lottery Ticket Hypothesis: Finding Sparse, Trainable Neural Networks
papers.research_problem: Why can heavily over-parameterized networks be pruned without losing accuracy?
storyline_structure: some subnetworks are trainable from initialization
papers.theory_pattern: Hypothesis-driven rather than theorem-driven.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A surprising empirical observation is supported by a clean protocol and repeated evidence.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.research_problem: Large language models struggled to expose and use intermediate reasoning for multi-step tasks.
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theorem; analysis focuses on scale and qualitative reasoning traces.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: The method is simple, but the evidence shows a robust qualitative shift in reasoning behavior.

papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
papers.research_problem: Reasoning and action in LLM-based agents had often been studied separately.
papers.theory_pattern: No central theorem; relies on behavioral and interpretability evidence.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A concise prompting paradigm addresses a clear agentic failure mode across diverse tasks.

papers.title: Wasserstein GAN
papers.research_problem: GAN training was unstable and losses were hard to interpret.
papers.theory_pattern: Formal analysis of probability distances motivates the algorithm.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A formal objective explains and improves a known training instability.

papers.title: Soft Actor-Critic: Off-Policy Maximum Entropy Deep Reinforcement Learning with a Stochastic Actor
papers.research_problem: Continuous-control RL needed sample-efficient, stable, and exploration-aware algorithms.
papers.theory_pattern: Maximum entropy RL objective and soft policy iteration motivate the algorithm.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A principled objective leads to a practical algorithm with strong sample efficiency.

papers.title: Certified Adversarial Robustness via Randomized Smoothing
papers.research_problem: Neural classifiers needed scalable certified robustness guarantees.
storyline_structure: randomized smoothing gives a certified classifier
papers.theory_pattern: Certification theorem is central and directly enables the evaluation procedure.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A clear theorem enables a scalable robustness certificate with measurable gains.

papers.title: Conservative Q-Learning for Offline Reinforcement Learning
papers.research_problem: Offline RL overestimates out-of-distribution actions and can learn unsafe policies.
papers.theory_pattern: Analysis connects conservative estimates to lower bounds on policy value.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: The algorithm directly targets a known offline RL failure and theory explains the conservative bias.

papers.title: Sharpness-Aware Minimization for Efficiently Improving Generalization
papers.research_problem: Deep models can fit training data but generalize poorly when solutions lie in sharp loss regions.
storyline_structure: generalization is tied to loss geometry
papers.theory_pattern: Generalization bound motivates minimizing both loss and sharpness.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A theoretically motivated objective gives consistent practical improvements with modest implementation changes.

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
papers.research_problem: Full fine-tuning of large language models is expensive in memory, storage, and deployment.
papers.theory_pattern: Empirical rank-deficiency analysis supports the design rather than a central theorem.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Strong efficiency gains are tied to a plausible structural property of fine-tuning updates.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.research_problem: Parametric language models have limited and hard-to-update factual memory.
papers.theory_pattern: Probabilistic latent-document formulation rather than theorem-centric proof.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A unified parametric/non-parametric memory view produces practical gains and a reusable pattern.

papers.title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
papers.research_problem: Offline RL can be reframed without value estimation and bootstrapping instability.
storyline_structure: RL is usually formulated as value learning
logical_flow: contrast RL and supervised sequence modeling
papers.theory_pattern: Conceptual reframing; no central theorem.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A surprising unification of RL and sequence modeling avoids known RL machinery and works empirically.

papers.title: A Generalist Agent
papers.research_problem: Different modalities and tasks were commonly handled by specialized agents or models.
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory; argument is architectural and empirical.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Breadth of tasks supports the generalist framing even when per-task optimality is not always the goal.

papers.title: GLUE: A Multi-Task Benchmark and Analysis Platform for Natural Language Understanding
papers.research_problem: The field needed a general, standardized evaluation for NLU systems.
storyline_structure: combine diverse tasks and diagnostic suite
papers.theory_pattern: No theory; value is benchmark design and analysis.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: The benchmark is broad, simple to use, and reveals limitations in current NLU systems.

papers.title: SuperGLUE: A Stickier Benchmark for General-Purpose Language Understanding Systems
papers.research_problem: Rapid progress saturated GLUE and demanded a harder general-purpose NLU benchmark.
storyline_structure: models approach or exceed old benchmark constraints
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A harder successor benchmark is justified by saturation and careful task construction.

papers.title: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding
papers.research_problem: Language models lacked a broad test of world knowledge and problem solving across many subjects.
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Broad subject coverage makes the benchmark a compelling proxy for multitask knowledge.

papers.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-world GitHub Issues?
papers.research_problem: Code LMs needed evaluation on realistic software issue resolution rather than isolated code snippets.
storyline_structure: use real GitHub issues and repositories
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Real-world provenance and low baseline performance show a meaningful gap for coding agents.

papers.title: Rethinking the Value of Network Pruning
papers.research_problem: It was unclear whether pruning's benefit came from finding compact architectures or preserving trained weights.
storyline_structure: compare pruned networks with training from scratch
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theorem.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A clean controlled comparison challenges a common assumption in model compression.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.research_problem: The relationship among model size, data, compute, and language modeling performance needed quantitative characterization.
papers.theory_pattern: Empirical power laws, not theorem-based proof.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Large controlled empirical grids yield actionable laws for future model training.

papers.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
papers.research_problem: Many LLMs were over-sized and under-trained relative to compute-optimal scaling.
papers.theory_pattern: Empirical scaling-law analysis.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A revised empirical law is validated by training a model that beats larger under-trained alternatives.

papers.title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
papers.research_problem: Language model capabilities, risks, and limitations were evaluated with fragmented tasks and metrics.
papers.theory_pattern: No theorem; contribution is evaluation design and empirical synthesis.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Breadth, standardization, and explicit incompleteness make the evaluation credible.

papers.title: Natural Questions: A Benchmark for Question Answering Research
papers.research_problem: Question answering datasets often used artificial questions or simplified evidence settings.
storyline_structure: QA should reflect real information needs
use anonymized Google queries and Wikipedia pages
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: Real user queries and realistic evidence pages make the task compelling.

papers.title: DROP: A Reading Comprehension Benchmark Requiring Discrete Reasoning Over Paragraphs
papers.research_problem: Reading comprehension systems needed evaluation on discrete reasoning rather than span matching alone.
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: The task isolates a missing reasoning capability and shows strong baselines struggle.

papers.title: Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems
papers.research_problem: Math word-problem reasoning required both task data and methods for selecting correct reasoning solutions.
papers.theory_pattern: No central theorem.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A concrete reasoning benchmark plus verifier method shows both the gap and a path forward.

papers.title: A Closer Look at Few-shot Classification
papers.research_problem: Few-shot learning methods were compared under inconsistent protocols, obscuring what actually mattered.
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theorem.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A fairer comparison changes the interpretation of progress in a crowded area.

papers.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
papers.research_problem: LLM agent capabilities needed systematic evaluation across interactive environments.
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theory.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A multi-environment comparison exposes agentic strengths and weaknesses that single-task tests miss.

papers.title: Robustness Gym: Unifying the NLP Evaluation Landscape
papers.research_problem: NLP robustness evaluations were fragmented across datasets, slices, and stress tests.
storyline_structure: unify evaluations through slices and evaluation sets
papers.theory_pattern: No formal theorem; contribution is evaluation abstraction.

analysis_experiments: robustness comparisons by subgroup or perturbation

papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: A unified evaluation abstraction makes robustness comparisons more systematic and reusable.

papers.title: Beyond the Imitation Game: Quantifying and Extrapolating the Capabilities of Language Models
papers.research_problem: Language model capabilities needed broad, diverse, and difficult tasks beyond narrow benchmark suites.
papers.theory_pattern: No central theorem; scaling extrapolation is empirical.
papers.reviewer_persuasion_strategy: The benchmark's breadth and difficulty support claims about general language model capabilities.
